critical.mandate: Every finding must be triaged — none may be left unprocessed
New stories created from findings must have status 'backlog'
Source story findings must be marked as triaged with the target story key or dismissed
New stories MUST be appended to epics-and-stories.md under the correct epic — the master planning artifact must stay in sync with story files
New story keys MUST use the next sequential number in the epic — scan existing stories to determine the last key. NEVER reuse an existing key.
New triaged stories MUST NOT be added to sprint-status.yaml — they are backlog items that get assigned to sprints via /gaia-sprint-plan or injected via /gaia-correct-course

step.title: Scan for Findings
step.action: Scan story files in {implementation_artifacts}/ for non-empty Findings tables

Collect all findings from ALL story files regardless of status — scan every .md file that has a non-empty Findings table. This ensures triage catches findings from stories in any status (backlog, validating, ready-for-dev, in-progress, blocked, review, done) so nothing is invisible to tech-debt-review later.
Skip findings already marked as [TRIAGED] or [DISMISSED]
If no untriaged findings found: inform user "No findings to triage" and stop

step.title: Present Findings
step.action: Group findings by severity (critical first, then high, medium, low)
For each finding, show: source story key, type, severity, description, suggested action
Present to user for triage decisions

step.title: Triage Each Finding
step.action: For each finding, generate a triage recommendation based on: - **Severity:** CRITICAL or HIGH findings → recommend CREATE STORY (standalone fix needed) - **Type:** 'tech-debt' and 'bug' findings → recommend CREATE STORY; 'improvement' findings → consider ADD TO EXISTING - **Scope:** If the finding is closely related to an existing backlog story → recommend ADD TO EXISTING and list candidate backlog stories for the user to pick from - **Relevance:** If the finding describes a temporary workaround that has since been resolved or the issue no longer applies → recommend DISMISS Present the recommendation with reasoning for each finding.

For each CREATE STORY recommendation, also recommend a timing/urgency: - **CRITICAL** severity (production bug, security vulnerability, data loss risk) → recommend **NOW**: "Inject into current sprint via /gaia-correct-course after triage completes" - **HIGH** severity (blocking debt, significant bug) → recommend **NEXT SPRINT**: flag as priority P0 so /gaia-sprint-plan picks it up first - **MEDIUM** severity → recommend **BACKLOG**: standard priority P1, pick up when capacity allows - **LOW** severity → recommend **BACKLOG**: standard priority P2, no urgency Present the timing recommendation alongside the triage decision.
In YOLO mode: auto-apply both the triage recommendation and the timing recommendation for each finding without prompting the user. Log each decision, reasoning, and timing.
Present the recommendation, then let the user confirm or override for each finding:

a. CREATE STORY — generate a new story file with status 'backlog', referencing the source story and finding. Use when: the finding represents a standalone piece of work (bug fix, tech debt cleanup, missing feature) that needs its own acceptance criteria and testing. After choosing CREATE STORY, confirm timing: [n] Now (inject into sprint) | [s] Next sprint (P0) | [b] Backlog (standard priority)

b. ADD TO EXISTING — append finding to an existing story's tasks. Use when: the finding is closely related to an existing backlog story and can be addressed as part of that work. When presenting candidate stories: only show stories with status 'backlog', 'validating', 'ready-for-dev', or 'in-progress'. EXCLUDE stories with status 'done' or 'review' — adding a finding to a completed story won't be worked on. If user tries to pick a done story: warn "Story {key} is already done. Adding a finding to a completed story won't be worked on. Choose a different story or use CREATE STORY instead."

c. DISMISS — finding is not actionable or already resolved. Use when: the finding was about a temporary state that no longer exists, the issue has been fixed by a subsequent change, or the risk is accepted.

step.title: Create Backlog Stories
step.action: For each CREATE STORY decision, determine the correct story key: 1. Identify which epic the finding belongs to — use the source story's epic_key (e.g., if source is E1-S3, the epic is E1) 2. Scan {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md AND {implementation_artifacts}/ for ALL existing stories in that epic 3. Find the highest story number in that epic (e.g., if E1-S1 through E1-S7 exist, the highest is S7) 4. Assign the next sequential key: E1-S8 5. NEVER reuse an existing key — always increment from the highest found across both sources

Generate a new story file using the story template: - Filename: {story_key}-{story_title_slug}.md - Set status to 'backlog' in YAML frontmatter (do NOT use status-sync — this is a new file, not an update) - Set priority based on timing decision: NOW → P0, NEXT SPRINT → P0, BACKLOG → P1 (medium) or P2 (low) - Link back to source story in Dependencies section - Use the finding's suggested action as the basis for the story's user story and acceptance criteria - Set risk level based on finding severity (CRITICAL→high, HIGH→high, MEDIUM→medium, LOW→low) - Do NOT add this story to sprint-status.yaml — it will be assigned to a sprint later via /gaia-sprint-plan or /gaia-correct-course

Update {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md: 1. Read the file and find the epic section for the new story's epic (e.g., ## Epic 1: ...) 2. Append the new story entry at the END of that epic's story list — after the last existing story in that epic 3. Include: story key, title, priority, size estimate, risk level, and a note "(from triage: {source_story_key})" 4. Do NOT insert at the beginning or middle of the list — always append at the end 5. If the epic section has a stories table: add a new row at the bottom 6. If the epic section has a stories list: add a new item at the bottom

step.title: Mark Findings as Triaged
step.action: In each source story's Findings table, update the processed findings:
- CREATE STORY: append "[TRIAGED → {new_story_key}]" to the Finding column
- ADD TO EXISTING: append "[TRIAGED → {existing_story_key}]" to the Finding column
- DISMISS: append "[DISMISSED]" to the Finding column

step.title: Summary and Recommendations
step.action: Present triage summary: total findings processed, stories created (with keys and priorities), items added to existing stories, dismissed
Confirm: "epics-and-stories.md updated with {N} new stories under their respective epics."

If any stories were marked as NOW (inject into current sprint): Present: "⚠ {count} story(ies) recommended for immediate sprint injection: {list of story keys with titles}. Run /gaia-correct-course to inject them into the current sprint."
If any stories were marked as NEXT SPRINT (P0): Present: "{count} story(ies) flagged as P0 for next sprint planning: {list of story keys}. They will be prioritized in /gaia-sprint-plan."

step.title: Save to Val Memory
